NOTIFIABLE MEDICAL CONDITIONS SURVEILLANCE SYSTEM

Author

The National Institute for Communicable Diseases

Published

April 1, 2026

Introduction

Data used in this report was drawn from the NMC-SS on 16 March 2026. The most recent report should always be viewed and can be found at NMCSS surveillance reports.

The purpose of this report is to describe the number of notifications received by the Notifiable Medical Conditions Surveillance System (NMCSS). The report is publicly available and can be used by health professionals, researchers, the general public, or any other stakeholder.

Category 4 NMCs, COVID-19, and multi-system inflammatory syndrome (MIS-C) have been excluded from this report. Where weeks are presented, the epiweek according to the CDC epiweeks are used.

Highlights

  • A total of 12 582 cases were notified in February, 2026 and most were category 2 conditions.
  • Category 1 cases were reported in a median (IQR) of 0 (0, 0) days.
  • The most common conditions notified were Acute flaccid paralysis, Acute rheumatic fever, Agricultural or stock remedy poisoning, Bilharzia (schistosomiasis), Brucellosis, Cholera, Congenital rubella syndrome, Congenital syphilis, Diphtheria, Endemic arboviral diseases west nile virus, Enteric fever (typhoid or paratyphoid fever), Food borne illness outbreak, Haemophilus influenzae type B, Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C, Hepatitis E, Lead poisoning, Legionellosis, Listeriosis, Malaria, Maternal death (pregnancy, childbirth and puerperium), Measles, Meningococcal disease, Mpox, Non-typhoidal salmonellosis, Pertussis, Rabies, Rubella, Shigellosis, Soil transmitted helminths, Tetanus, Tuberculosis: extensively drug -resistant (XDR -TB), Tuberculosis: multidrug- resistant (MDR -TB), Tuberculosis:extra-pulmonary, Tuberculosis:pulmonary.

Category 1 Conditions

Category 1 conditions must be notified within 24 hours of diagnosis. A total of 2 005 category 1 conditions were notified in February, 2026. The majority were notified with a median (IQR) time to notification of 0 (0, 0) days.

NMC Reporting Application

  • NMC Reporting App is available on both web and mobile platforms.
  • Use recommended browsers to access the NMC reporting App for notifications, searching of cases and reports.
  • Register if you have no NMC account; you can reset your password if you have not used the application in over 12 months.

All Category 1 Conditions at a Glance

NMC Data Summary, February, 2026

In February, 2026 the NMCSS received a total of 12 450 notifications. Of these, 2 005 (16%) were Category 1 notifications, 10 351 (83%) were Category 2, and 94 (0.8%) were Category 3.

Category 1 Notifications

The most commonly notified category 1 condition was Malaria (836 (42%)).

Category 2 Notifications

Category 2 conditions must be notified within 7 days of diagnosis.

The most common Category 2 condition was Tuberculosis:pulmonary (6 616 (64%)).

Data Quality

Completeness

ID Number Completeness

Timeliness

Conclusion

The majority of notifications were clinical notifications. Patients who are hospitalised with a category 1 condition and notified still have poor completeness of the hospital form, with the majority of notifications only having symptoms completed. ID numbers are poorly completed in notifications from SDW.

Recommendations

  • We recommend that clinicians complete all patient clinical and demographic details to improve hospital form completeness.
  • We strongly recommend complete ID number capture in the SDW system to improve data quality and the ability for the NMCSS to merge clinical and laboratory notifications.
  • Feedback and suggestions are welcome at brianb@nicd.ac.za and matimbam@nicd.ac.za.

Appendices

Appendix 1: Back-Captured Clinical Notifications

Appendix 2: Summary of NMCSS Data Flow

The NMCSS receives notifications from:

  1. NMC App — web, iOS, Android (clinical and laboratory notifications)
  2. Paper forms — submitted by facilities and captured into the system
  3. SDW/Microstrategy — laboratory notifications drawn from the NHLS system

Notifications are merged where clinical and laboratory notifications relate to the same case.

Appendix 3: NMC Categories and Case Classification

Category Notification period Description
Category 1 Within 24 hours Immediately notifiable; notify on clinical suspicion
Category 2 Within 7 days Routine surveillance; notify after diagnosis
Category 3 Within 7 days Outbreak-prone; aggregate reporting
Category 4 Excluded from this report
